title: The Miser & His Gold
content:
He buried his Gold in a hole.
    One saw, and the treasure he stole.
    Said another, "What matter?
    Don't raise such a clatter,
    You can still go & sit by the hole."

    Use Alone Gives Value
meaning:
"The Miser & His Gold" is a quintessential example of Walter Crane's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
